nānujānanti.meaning: they do not agree; they do not consent to
nānujānanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, negated (na + anujānāti, √ñā)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To know; anujānāti means to consent, permit.

nānusocanti.meaning: they do not grieve; they do not wail or moan
nānusocanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, negated (na + anusocati, √suc)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To grieve; anusocati means to mourn over, wail.

nānābhāvo.meaning: separation; becoming different, diversity (of all kinds, various)
nānābhāvo.gram: masculine abstract noun, compound, nominative singular

nānābāhāsu.meaning: by many arms; among the various arms

nānākulā.meaning: of different families; from various clans

nāparaṁ.meaning: nothing further; not another, no more
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).

nāpasādaye.meaning: should not disparage; would not blame or put down
nāpasādaye.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative, causative, negated (na + apasādeti)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To disparage, put down, dismiss (causative of apa + √sad).

nārādhako.meaning: not one who accomplishes; one who does not succeed (in the practice)
nārādhako.gram: masculine noun, negated, nominative singular (na + ārādhaka)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
One who accomplishes, succeeds, wins favour (from ārādheti, √rādh).

nātimaññetha.meaning: should not despise; let one not look down upon
nātimaññetha.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative/imperative middle, negated (na + atimaññati, √man)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).
To think; atimaññati means to despise, to think oneself above another.

nāvattena.meaning: not by falling back; without turning back to
breakdown: Not (fused with the following ā- to nā-).

nāññamaññassa.meaning: not of one another; not to each other
nāññamaññassa.gram: sandhi fusion: negative + reciprocal pronoun (na + aññamaññassa)
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).

nāññatra.meaning: not otherwise than; not except, nowhere else
breakdown: Not (fused with the following vowel to nā-).

nīce.gram: adjective, locative singular or plural form of nīca

nīlakācamaṇisannibhasilātalaṁ.meaning: a rock surface resembling a dark-blue glass-like gem
nīlakācamaṇisannibhasilātalaṁ.summary: This is a long descriptive compound: "dark blue + glasslike + gem + resembling + rock + surface".

nīvaraṇa.meaning: hindrance; obstacle (especially one of the five hindrances)
breakdown: Prefix: down, back (lengthened to nī- in this derivative).
To obstruct, hold back; nīvaraṇa is that which obstructs the mind.
nīvaraṇa.goenka: In the study material nīvaraṇa is the hindrance or obstacle, and the five hindrances (pañca nīvaraṇāni) are listed as sensual desire, ill will, sloth and torpor, agitation and feeling of remorse, and all kinds of doubt — kāmacchando, byāpādo, thīnamiddha, uddhaccakukkucca, vicikicchā. These are the five enemies the meditator must learn to master in practice; Klaus Nothnagel refers readers to lesson 3.7.8, Nīvaraṇapabbaṁ — Mastering the Hindrances, for the full treatment.

obhaṭacumbaṭā.meaning: one whose head-cloth has been taken away; with the head-pad removed
breakdown: Taken away, removed (past participle of obharati).
Head-cloth, the coiled pad placed on the head for carrying loads.
To shine; obhāseti means to illuminate.

obhāso.meaning: light, radiance; here: showing, indicating (an improper hint for gains)
obhāso.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (from obhāsati, ava + √bhās)
breakdown: To shine; obhāsa is shining, light; figuratively, giving a broad hint.

odapattakinī.meaning: a wife by the water-bowl ceremony; one given in marriage by dipping the hand in water
odapattakinī.note: divergence: the source explains the term as udakapattaṁ āmasitvā vāseti, "she lives with her hand having touched the water (bowl)"; Bhikkhu Bodhi renders it "given (in marriage with the ceremony of) dipping the hand in water".

odātamanasaṅkappo.meaning: with clean mind and intentions; of pure thoughts

ogha.meaning: flood; figuratively, the flood of ignorance (that sweeps beings along)
breakdown: Flood, torrent; in the suttas a figure for the currents of sensuality, becoming, views and ignorance that carry beings through saṁsāra.

ohitabhāro.meaning: one who has laid down the burden
breakdown: Laid down, put down (past participle of ava + √dhā).
ohitabhāro.goenka: Ohitabhāro, "the burden laid down", is one of the stock epithets of the Arahant: one who has lived the holy life, done what had to be done, and laid down the burden of the five aggregates — the load of mental and physical conditioning that an unliberated being keeps carrying.

ohitabhārā.meaning: those who have laid down the burden
breakdown: Laid down, put down (past participle of ava + √dhā).

ohāretvā.meaning: having removed; having cut off (e.g. hair and beard)
breakdown: To carry; ohāreti means to remove, cut off.

okappessati.meaning: will put one's trust in; will settle confidence upon
okappessati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of okappeti (ava + √kapp)
breakdown: To fix, arrange; okappeti means to settle on, put trust in.

okassa.meaning: of the house; of the dwelling (also: of attachment to home)
okassa.gram: masculine/neuter noun, genitive singular of oka
breakdown: House, dwelling, abode; figuratively the "home" of attachment.

okkamamānā.gram: present participle of okkamati (ava + √kam), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Prefix: down. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To step; okkamati means to descend, fall into, enter.
Prefix: down. (The doubled kk arises by assimilation.)
To step; okkamati means to step down into, enter.

okkanti.meaning: descent; falling down, entry (e.g. into the womb, conception)

okoṭimako.meaning: of ugly appearance; deformed (literally "having the top lowered", dwarfish)

breakdown: To do, make; okāra is what is brought low — vileness, worthlessness.

omakkhiṁ karonti.gram: phrase: adjective + verb, 3rd person plural present
breakdown: They make, they do (3rd person plural of karoti, √kar).

onamissati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of onamati (ava + √nam)
breakdown: To bend; onamati means to bend down.

opaneyyiko.meaning: leading straight to the goal; to be brought within, leading onward
breakdown: Suffixes forming "fit to lead one on".
opaneyyiko.goenka: Opaneyyiko is one of the six qualities of the Dhamma recited in the formula of Dhamma reflection: the teaching is sandiṭṭhiko (to be seen here and now), akāliko (timeless, giving results without delay), ehipassiko (inviting one to come and see), opaneyyiko (leading straight to the goal), and paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhi (to be experienced directly by the wise, each for oneself). The emphasis is experiential: the Dhamma leads onward whoever actually walks the path.

breakdown: To fall, alight; upapāta is rebirth by spontaneous arising.

orabbhiko.meaning: butcher (of sheep); one who slaughters rams
orabbhiko.gram: masculine agent noun, nominative singular (from urabbha "ram")
breakdown: Agent suffix: one who deals in, a slaughterer of.

orambhāgiyānaṁ.meaning: belonging to the lower (world); of the lower kind (said of the five lower fetters)
breakdown: Below, on this side, inferior. (The m is a junction consonant.)
Belonging to a part or share (from bhāga "part, section").

orima.meaning: the lowest; on this side, the nearer (shore)

orimatīrasuttaṁ.meaning: the Discourse on the Nearer Shore (Orimatīrasutta)
orimatīrasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter; title of a sutta
breakdown: This side, the nearer; the lowest.
To climb; orohati means to climb down, descend.
To move, flow; osarati means to go to, enter.

ottappa.meaning: moral dread; sense of guilt, anxiety about evil that could cause blame
breakdown: To be tormented, scorched; ottappa is the wholesome dread of wrongdoing.
ottappa.goenka: Ottappa is explained in the source as having dread of blame as its characteristic: anxiety about evil that could cause blame, citing the commentarial definition tehiyeva ottappatīti ottappaṁ — pāpato ubbegassetaṁ adhivacanaṁ, uttāsanalakkhaṇaṁ ottappaṁ. Together with hiri (moral shame), it is one of the two guardians of the world: hiri recoils from evil out of self-respect, ottappa out of dread of its consequences and of blame.

otāretvā.meaning: having brought down; having taken down
breakdown: To cross; the causative otāreti means to cause to come down, take down.

ovadanti.meaning: they instruct; they exhort, they advise
ovadanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of ovadati (ava + √vad)
breakdown: To speak; ovadati means to advise, exhort.
To speak; ovadati means to advise, admonish, instruct.

ovadeyyānusāseyya.meaning: should admonish and advise; would exhort and instruct
ovadeyyānusāseyya.gram: sandhi fusion of two optative verbs (ovadeyya + anusāseyya)
breakdown: Should admonish, exhort (optative of ovadati, ava + √vad).
Should advise, instruct (optative of anusāsati, anu + √sās). (The two optatives fuse by vowel coalescence.)
